toolUseResult.prompt:
Spec a small follow-up task AAR-0008B that addresses the 3 low-risk findings you raised in your own code review of AAR-0008. All three must be fixed before we commit, not after. The fixes will be bundled into the same commit as AAR-0008.

Task doc location: `vault/studio/projects/autonomous-agent-research/tasks/` — name it following the existing pattern. Suggested filename: `2026-04-07-HHmmSS-AAR-0008B-review-findings.md` (use the getdate tool for the timestamp). Also update `TASKS.md` in the same folder to register AAR-0008B after AAR-0008 and before AAR-0009.

## The 3 findings (from your own review)

### Finding 1 — Migration tool can over-archive on re-runs

`src/tools/migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts` indiscriminately archives all `*.md` files in each agent's `staged/` directory. If the user runs the migration after a NEW pipeline run has produced correct (title-bearing) staged files, those correct files would be archived alongside the pre-fix corrupted ones.

**Fix:** The migration tool must filter files by absence of the `title:` field in frontmatter. Pre-fix files have no title field. Post-fix files always have one (the AAR-0008 writer always emits it). Use a simple regex scan of the frontmatter block — do NOT introduce a YAML parser dependency.

**Acceptance:**
- Dry-run output lists only files that lack `title:` in frontmatter
- Running after a mixed dir (77 pre-fix + 5 post-fix) archives exactly 77 files, leaves the 5 post-fix files untouched
- Tool exits cleanly (zero count) if run on a dir with only post-fix files
- Error handling: files with malformed frontmatter (no `---` delimiters) should be treated as pre-fix and archived (the corrupted state)

### Finding 2 — `brain --search-staged` CLI display doesn't surface title

`src/tools/brain.ts` has two display sites (around lines 661 and 709) that preview staged proposals as `r.proposedChange.slice(0, 80)`. The `title` field is available in `StagedResult` after AAR-0008 but unused.

**Fix:** Update both display sites to show title as the primary label, with content as a secondary snippet. Proposed format:

```
[pending] penny/Signature Moves — Daily Log Style
  Content: Brief formatting guidelines for daily notes emphasizing...
  Reason: Judges rejected: gemini
  → memory/penny/staged/20260407-094337-daily-log-style.md
```

(Title in the header line, short content snippet on a secondary line, reason, path.)

If title is empty (shouldn't happen post-fix but defensive), fall back to a 80-char content snippet as the header.

**Acceptance:**
- Running `brain --search-staged "foo"` shows title in every result header
- Content snippet is capped at 100 chars with ellipsis
- Empty-title results gracefully fall back to content-only display
- No other CLI output paths broken

### Finding 3 — YAML escape only handles double-quotes

`src/libs/staged.ts` renders frontmatter values via `.replace(/"/g, "'")`. This is insufficient for:
- Embedded newlines (breaks the parseFm regex which splits on `\n`)
- Backslashes
- Mixed quote types

Currently this is safe because Gemini's `=== PROPOSAL N: {title} ===` format is guaranteed single-line, and `reason` comes from code-generated strings. But the schema doesn't enforce this — a future change to the pipeline could introduce multi-line reasons or titles.

**Fix:** Make frontmatter round-tripping robust using one of these approaches (you choose, justify in the ADR):

**Option A — Strict single-line normalization.** Strip `\n` and `\r` from values before writing. Collapse runs of whitespace to single space. Replace `"` with `'`. Cheapest, preserves current parseFm contract. Downside: lossy if multi-line values ever matter.

**Option B — JSON.stringify writer + parseFm upgrade.** Write values via `JSON.stringify(value)`, which handles all escaping correctly and produces a valid YAML 1.2 flow-style string. Upgrade `parseFm` in `src/libs/staged.ts` to detect JSON-encoded values (leading `"`, parse via `JSON.parse`) and fall back to the existing quote-strip for legacy files. Handles all edge cases; also makes frontmatter robust for future fields.

**Option C — YAML block scalars** (`|`). Uses proper YAML multi-line syntax. Requires a real YAML parser. Introduces a dependency. Reject this option.

**Recommendation:** I lean Option B for durability. Option A is acceptable if you judge the complexity not worth it. Do NOT pick Option C. Justify your choice in the task doc ADR.

**Acceptance:**
- A staged proposal with multi-line title or reason round-trips correctly through write → read
- Legacy files (no `title:` field) still parse via the existing fallback path
- Round-trip test from AAR-0008 still passes
- Add one new round-trip test case specifically for the chosen escape scheme

## Constraints

- Sequential only — Ryan will run this after AAR-0008 implementation is already on disk (not yet committed). The code is there, he builds on it.
- File scope should be minimal: `migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts`, `brain.ts`, `staged.ts` (if Option B), and the test file. Confirm by grep before writing the spec.
- Bundle into the AAR-0008 commit — no separate commit. Note this in the task doc sequencing section so Ryan knows.
- Follow the same format as AAR-0008: file-by-file spec, function signatures, acceptance criteria, ADR section for decisions.
- Keep it tight — this is a follow-up, not a new feature.

## What to report

- Task doc path
- Your Option choice for finding 3 and one-sentence justification
- Updated TASKS.md entry
- Any bad intelligence in your original review findings you want to correct
- Any additional risks you spotted while writing this spec

Do not dispatch Ryan. Do not implement. Task doc design only.

content.text:
`readdirSync` returns names as strings (not Dirent by default). `archive-pre-fix` is a dir name with no `.md` extension so it's filtered out by `.endsWith(".md")`. Clean.

But wait — subfolder scanning. `readdirSync(stagedDir)` is non-recursive — it lists only immediate children. Files inside `archive-pre-fix/` are not iterated, so they aren't re-archived. Idempotent re-run safe.

Let me also confirm the round-trip test cleanup doesn't risk deleting the real `memory/` root. The `rmSync(join(stagedDir, ".."))` call. `stagedDir = vault/studio/memory/__roundtrip_escape_test__/staged`, so `stagedDir/..` = `vault/studio/memory/__roundtrip_escape_test__` — only the synthetic agent dir, not `memory/`. Safe.

Now let me also consider the cross-cutting issue: when brain --check runs after a new JSON-encoded file lands, sync.ts reads, its legacy parser strips outer quotes but leaves internal escapes as literals. Here is the concrete risk summary:

1. Simple ASCII titles with no quotes or newlines: OK (brain.db matches disk)
2. Titles with embedded `"`: CORRUPT in brain.db (`\"` literal remains)
3. Titles with `\n` newlines: CORRUPT in brain.db (`\n` literal remains, never newline)
4. Titles with `\\` backslashes: CORRUPT in brain.db (doubled backslashes)
5. Morning briefing: OK (reads disk via staged.ts parseFm)
6. brain --search-staged: CORRUPT on above cases (reads from brain.db via searchStaged → SQL)
7. brain --recall-all: CORRUPT on above cases (same path)

Practical test: does today's Gemini output contain these? Looking at existing staged files, reasons are simple and sections are enum-like. No current corruption. But the Cloudflare constraint in the task doc says "pin it so the contract is enforced by CI, not hope" — and sync.ts is one of the enforcement points.

### Per-dimension findings

#### Finding 1 — Migration filter: PASS

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\tools\migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts`

1. `isPreFixFile(filePath: string): boolean` exists at lines 27-43. Correct signature (takes full path, not just content — spec said `content: string` in header but the pseudocode in the spec passed filePath; Ryan chose filePath, which is fine and avoids an extra read at the call site).
2. Detection logic correct: frontmatter must have `---` delimiters (line 35) AND must contain `^title\s*:` within the captured FM block (line 42). Missing either → returns `true` (pre-fix).
3. Main loop calls `isPreFixFile` at line 69, before the archive loop.
4. Dry-run output correctly reports both counts: `DRY-RUN src -> dst` per archived file (line 80), plus `(skipped N post-fix file(s))` summary (lines 84-86).
5. Regex used, no YAML parser. `^title\s*:` with `m` flag.
6. Edge cases verified:
   - Empty file → no regex match → returns true (archive). OK.
   - `---\n---` with nothing between → regex requires `\r?\n` between delimiters → no match → returns true. OK.
   - Frontmatter with no title → regex matches FM, inner test fails → returns true. OK.
   - `title:` in body only → non-greedy `[\s\S]*?` stops FM at first `---`, body is outside captured group → inner test fails → returns true. OK.
   - `title: "foo"` on first line of FM → `^title\s*:` matches → returns false (skip). OK.
7. `--apply` path uses the same filter — the filter is applied to `files` at line 69, and both dry-run and apply iterate the same `files` array. Single source of truth.

Additional edge case I verified: the `archive-pre-fix/` subdirectory created by `--apply` is non-`.md` and is excluded by the `.endsWith(".md")` filter at line 68, so re-running on an already-migrated dir is idempotent.

#### Finding 2 — brain CLI display: PASS

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\tools\brain.ts`

2. Both sites updated (lines 670 and 719). No lingering `r.proposedChange.slice(0, 80)` in the file — grep confirmed only the three references all go through `formatStagedSnippet`.
3. Format matches spec:
   - Site 1 (recall-all, line 670): dense one-line `[status] agent/section — label` preserved as spec intended.
   - Site 2 (search-staged, lines 719-725): 4-line layout with header, conditional Content, Reason, path arrow.
4. Content snippet capped at 100 chars via `formatStagedSnippet(r.proposedChange, 100)` at line 722.
5. Empty-title fallback works — `r.title || formatStagedSnippet(r.proposedChange, 80)` at both sites, and the `Content:` line at line 721 is gated on `if (r.title)` so when title is empty (fallback header is already the content snippet), no redundant Content line prints.
6. `--recall-all` call site (line 670) uses title. Confirmed.
7. `--search-staged` call site (line 719) uses title. Confirmed.
8. No other display sites touched. Grep for `proposedChange` returns only these three lines.

#### Finding 3 — Frontmatter escape (Option B): PASS (with caveat — see Cross-cutting)

1. `fmEscape(value: string): string` at lines 47-49. Uses `JSON.stringify` internally.
2. `writeStagedProposal()` calls `fmEscape` for `title` (line 147), `section` (line 148), `reason` (line 151). All three user-controllable fields covered.
3. No old `.replace(/"/g, "'")` remains. Grep confirmed. `safeTitle` variable gone.
4. `parseFm()` upgraded correctly:

   - Falls back to quote-strip on parse failure for legacy compatibility (lines 82-84).
   - Separate `else if` branch for single-quoted legacy values (line 85).
5. Line-splitting behavior unchanged: `match[1].split(/\r?\n/)` at line 71, same as before. JSON escapes like `\n` stay as two-char literals in the source, don't introduce real newlines.
6. Interaction verified: JSON.stringify encodes real `\n` as `\\n` (two chars), source file line stays single-line, parseFm reads that line, JSON.parse decodes back to a real newline. Round-trip test pins this.

### BLOCKER: Cross-cutting regression in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\libs\brain\sync.ts:700-714`

`syncStaged()` has its own inline frontmatter parser that was NOT updated to handle JSON-encoded values. It still runs the AAR-0005-era quote-strip logic:

```typescript
// sync.ts:709
if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
  val = val.slice(1, -1);
}
```

**Concrete corruption paths for any staged file with special characters:**

| Input field (via writeStagedProposal) | On-disk source line | disk parseFm (new, correct) | sync.ts parse (old, bug) | brain.db stored |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Title with "quotes"` | `title: "Title with \"quotes\""` | `Title with "quotes"` | `Title with \"quotes\"` | CORRUPT |
| `Line 1\nLine 2` (real newline) | `reason: "Line 1\nLine 2"` (literal `\n`) | `Line 1` + LF + `Line 2` | `Line 1\nLine 2` (literal backslash-n) | CORRUPT |
| `path\to\thing` (backslashes) | `section: "path\\to\\thing"` | `path\to\thing` | `path\\to\\thing` | CORRUPT |
| Simple ASCII with no metachars | `title: "Simple title"` | `Simple title` | `Simple title` | OK (lucky) |

**Impact:**
- Morning briefing (`morning.ts → getPendingProposals → listStagedProposals`) reads disk via the upgraded parseFm. Displays correctly.
- `brain --search-staged` (`brain.ts:702 → queries.ts:searchStaged → staged_proposals table`) reads brain.db which was populated by sync.ts's legacy parser. Displays corrupt values.
- `brain --recall-all` (same path). Displays corrupt values.

**Practical risk today:** Low. All observed Gemini outputs are single-line ASCII with no embedded quotes in `title` or `reason`. Existing enum-like values for `section` and `reason` don't trigger the bug. The round-trip test passes because it only exercises the disk path (`writeStagedProposal` → `listStagedProposals`), which both go through the new parseFm.

**Latent risk (matches the exact threat Finding 3 was meant to neutralize):** The moment AAR-0009 or any downstream change introduces a multi-line `reason` or a title with embedded quotes, brain.db silently stores corrupted values while disk stays clean. The two views of the same data diverge. Reviewers using `brain --search-staged` see escape artifacts; the morning briefing doesn't. Hard to debug, trivial to avoid by fixing sync.ts now.

**This is bad intelligence in my own task doc.** The "Out of Scope" section explicitly said:

> Touching `syncStaged()` in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`. The orphan-removal pass already handles archived files correctly because the `staged/` scan is non-recursive and skips subdirectories.

The out-of-scope entry addressed orphan-removal but silently assumed sync.ts's frontmatter parser was the same code path as staged.ts's parseFm. It isn't — sync.ts has its own copy, and it wasn't updated. This is a duplicated-logic hazard I failed to notice when writing the doc. Ryan followed the doc faithfully; the gap is mine.

---

### Required fix

Sync.ts lines 700-714 need the same parser upgrade that landed in staged.ts:

- Detect leading `"` → try JSON.parse → fall back to legacy strip
- OR (cleaner) refactor to call `parseFm` from `staged.ts` directly. Currently `parseFm` is module-private. Either export it or mirror the logic.

**Recommendation:** Export `parseFm` from `staged.ts` as `parseStagedFrontmatter` and import it in `sync.ts`. Single source of truth. Prevents this exact bug from recurring.

**Scope of the fix:** 1 file, ~10 lines changed. Still bundles into the AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B single commit.

---

### Cross-cutting checks

1. **Other frontmatter parsers:** `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` (`parseFrontmatter`) is used by the broader sync pipeline for memories/references/projects but NOT for staged proposals. Those files still use YAML-ish formats written by humans, not the JSON-encoded writer. Not affected.
2. **Backwards compatibility (new reader + old files):** Verified. Legacy files with single-quoted values hit the `else if (val.startsWith("'"))` branch. Legacy files with double-quoted simple values (no escapes) pass through JSON.parse cleanly because `"simple title"` IS valid JSON.
3. **Forwards compatibility (old reader + new files):** THIS is the sync.ts hole. Any future parser that copies the legacy pattern will break on JSON-encoded values.
4. **`bun build` typecheck:** Clean for all four touched files. No type errors.
5. **`bun test staged-roundtrip.test.ts`:** 2 pass, 0 fail, 12 expect() calls. Runs cleanly on Windows despite the known segfault issue — no import-from-modified-modules pattern triggered.

---

### Regression risks Ryan didn't catch

Ryan can't be faulted for missing the sync.ts gap because:
1. The task doc explicitly marked sync.ts out of scope for this task.
2. The failure path is latent today — current data doesn't exercise it.
3. Ryan's own tests pass because they only exercise the disk path, which is what the task doc told him to test.

Nothing else flagged. Implementation is otherwise tight.

---

### Single-commit bundle readiness

NOT READY. Fix sync.ts first, then bundle AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B + the sync.ts fix into one commit. Commit message should reference all three: `AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B: staged content bug fix + review findings + sync parser upgrade` (or similar).

Rationale for bundling the sync.ts fix into the same commit rather than a new task:
- It's ~10 lines in one file.
- It's a direct consequence of Finding 3 landing (JSON-encoded writer).
- Shipping AAR-0008B without it leaves a latent corruption path that undermines the whole point of Finding 3.
- A separate AAR-0008C would mean three task IDs for what is functionally one cleanup pass.

---

### Overall risk assessment

| Dimension | Risk | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Correctness (disk path) | LOW | Round-trip test pins it. All three findings landed. |
| Correctness (DB path) | MEDIUM | Sync parser bug. Latent today, inevitable tomorrow if AAR-0009 touches pipeline. |

| Test coverage | MEDIUM | Disk path is pinned; DB path is not. A sync-to-DB round-trip test would catch the sync.ts bug. Not required for this commit, but worth a follow-up. |
| Blast radius | LOW | All changes confined to 4 files + 1 sync.ts fix. No type changes, no schema changes, no new deps. |
| Idempotency | LOW-RISK | Migration tool is idempotent by design. Filter is deterministic on content. |

`readdirSync` returns names as strings (not Dirent by default). `archive-pre-fix` is a dir name with no `.md` extension so it's filtered out by `.endsWith(".md")`. Clean.

But wait — subfolder scanning. `readdirSync(stagedDir)` is non-recursive — it lists only immediate children. Files inside `archive-pre-fix/` are not iterated, so they aren't re-archived. Idempotent re-run safe.

Let me also confirm the round-trip test cleanup doesn't risk deleting the real `memory/` root. The `rmSync(join(stagedDir, ".."))` call. `stagedDir = vault/studio/memory/__roundtrip_escape_test__/staged`, so `stagedDir/..` = `vault/studio/memory/__roundtrip_escape_test__` — only the synthetic agent dir, not `memory/`. Safe.

Now let me also consider the cross-cutting issue: when brain --check runs after a new JSON-encoded file lands, sync.ts reads, its legacy parser strips outer quotes but leaves internal escapes as literals. Here is the concrete risk summary:

1. Simple ASCII titles with no quotes or newlines: OK (brain.db matches disk)
2. Titles with embedded `"`: CORRUPT in brain.db (`\"` literal remains)
3. Titles with `\n` newlines: CORRUPT in brain.db (`\n` literal remains, never newline)
4. Titles with `\\` backslashes: CORRUPT in brain.db (doubled backslashes)
5. Morning briefing: OK (reads disk via staged.ts parseFm)
6. brain --search-staged: CORRUPT on above cases (reads from brain.db via searchStaged → SQL)
7. brain --recall-all: CORRUPT on above cases (same path)

Practical test: does today's Gemini output contain these? Looking at existing staged files, reasons are simple and sections are enum-like. No current corruption. But the Cloudflare constraint in the task doc says "pin it so the contract is enforced by CI, not hope" — and sync.ts is one of the enforcement points.

## Verdict

Time to report.

---

## Review Report: AAR-0008B

### Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED (one blocker)

Ryan's implementation is faithful to the task doc on all three findings and all acceptance criteria in the doc are met. The blocker is a gap I missed in the task doc itself, not a Ryan deviation.

---

### Per-dimension findings

#### Finding 1 — Migration filter: PASS

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\tools\migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts`

1. `isPreFixFile(filePath: string): boolean` exists at lines 27-43. Correct signature (takes full path, not just content — spec said `content: string` in header but the pseudocode in the spec passed filePath; Ryan chose filePath, which is fine and avoids an extra read at the call site).
2. Detection logic correct: frontmatter must have `---` delimiters (line 35) AND must contain `^title\s*:` within the captured FM block (line 42). Missing either → returns `true` (pre-fix).
3. Main loop calls `isPreFixFile` at line 69, before the archive loop.
4. Dry-run output correctly reports both counts: `DRY-RUN src -> dst` per archived file (line 80), plus `(skipped N post-fix file(s))` summary (lines 84-86).
5. Regex used, no YAML parser. `^title\s*:` with `m` flag.
6. Edge cases verified:
   - Empty file → no regex match → returns true (archive). OK.
   - `---\n---` with nothing between → regex requires `\r?\n` between delimiters → no match → returns true. OK.
   - Frontmatter with no title → regex matches FM, inner test fails → returns true. OK.
   - `title:` in body only → non-greedy `[\s\S]*?` stops FM at first `---`, body is outside captured group → inner test fails → returns true. OK.
   - `title: "foo"` on first line of FM → `^title\s*:` matches → returns false (skip). OK.
7. `--apply` path uses the same filter — the filter is applied to `files` at line 69, and both dry-run and apply iterate the same `files` array. Single source of truth.

Additional edge case I verified: the `archive-pre-fix/` subdirectory created by `--apply` is non-`.md` and is excluded by the `.endsWith(".md")` filter at line 68, so re-running on an already-migrated dir is idempotent.

#### Finding 2 — brain CLI display: PASS

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\tools\brain.ts`

1. `formatStagedSnippet()` helper at lines 53-57. Handles newline collapse, trim, and ellipsis cap correctly.
2. Both sites updated (lines 670 and 719). No lingering `r.proposedChange.slice(0, 80)` in the file — grep confirmed only the three references all go through `formatStagedSnippet`.
3. Format matches spec:
   - Site 1 (recall-all, line 670): dense one-line `[status] agent/section — label` preserved as spec intended.
   - Site 2 (search-staged, lines 719-725): 4-line layout with header, conditional Content, Reason, path arrow.
4. Content snippet capped at 100 chars via `formatStagedSnippet(r.proposedChange, 100)` at line 722.
5. Empty-title fallback works — `r.title || formatStagedSnippet(r.proposedChange, 80)` at both sites, and the `Content:` line at line 721 is gated on `if (r.title)` so when title is empty (fallback header is already the content snippet), no redundant Content line prints.
6. `--recall-all` call site (line 670) uses title. Confirmed.
7. `--search-staged` call site (line 719) uses title. Confirmed.
8. No other display sites touched. Grep for `proposedChange` returns only these three lines.

#### Finding 3 — Frontmatter escape (Option B): PASS (with caveat — see Cross-cutting)

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\libs\staged.ts`

1. `fmEscape(value: string): string` at lines 47-49. Uses `JSON.stringify` internally.
2. `writeStagedProposal()` calls `fmEscape` for `title` (line 147), `section` (line 148), `reason` (line 151). All three user-controllable fields covered.
3. No old `.replace(/"/g, "'")` remains. Grep confirmed. `safeTitle` variable gone.
4. `parseFm()` upgraded correctly:
   - Detects leading `"` (line 78).
   - Tries `JSON.parse` (line 80).
   - Falls back to quote-strip on parse failure for legacy compatibility (lines 82-84).
   - Separate `else if` branch for single-quoted legacy values (line 85).
5. Line-splitting behavior unchanged: `match[1].split(/\r?\n/)` at line 71, same as before. JSON escapes like `\n` stay as two-char literals in the source, don't introduce real newlines.
6. Interaction verified: JSON.stringify encodes real `\n` as `\\n` (two chars), source file line stays single-line, parseFm reads that line, JSON.parse decodes back to a real newline. Round-trip test pins this.
7. Error handling: try/catch at lines 79-84 prevents JSON.parse throws. Malformed JSON falls back to legacy strip.
8. listStagedProposals fallback chain (lines 232-235) intact: `fm.title → h1Match → ""`. Still works with new parseFm.

#### Round-trip test: PASS

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\libs\__tests__\staged-roundtrip.test.ts`

1. New test uses `__roundtrip_escape_test__` (line 53), distinct from `__roundtrip_test__` (line 13). No collision.
2. Test content exercises:
   - Embedded double quotes in title (line 61): `'Title with "quotes" and a colon: tricky'`
   - Embedded quotes in section (line 64)
   - Multi-line reason with real newlines AND embedded escaped quote (line 68): `"Multi-line reason:\nLine two of the reason\nLine three with a \"quoted\" word"`
   - Colon inside title (tests that parseFm's `indexOf(":")` doesn't split on it, because the value starts with `"` and JSON.parse consumes the whole quoted segment)
3. Round-trip assertion uses `expect(out.x).toBe(input.x)` which is strict byte equality for primitives. Lines 77-80.
4. `finally` cleanup at lines 81-85 removes the synthetic agent dir only (`stagedDir/..` = the test-agent dir, not the memory root).
5. Pre-existing AAR-0008 test unchanged — verified by reading lines 10-48. Both tests pass under `bun test`:

```
2 pass
0 fail
12 expect() calls
```

#### AAR-0008 preservation: PASS

1. `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts:625-626` — `title: proposal.title, proposedChange: proposal.content` preserved. Verified.
2. `src/libs/brain/index.ts:282-307` — title column migration + FTS5 rebuild block intact. Verified.
3. `src/libs/brain/queries.ts:1240-1300` — `searchStaged` returns title in StagedResult. Verified.
4. `src/tools/morning.ts:190` — `p.title || (p.proposedChange.split(/\r?\n/)[0])` title-first display intact. Verified.
5. `StagedProposal` interface (staged.ts:19-31) — `title: string` as first-class required field, comment preserved. Verified.

---

### BLOCKER: Cross-cutting regression in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`

File: `D:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\src\libs\brain\sync.ts:700-714`

`syncStaged()` has its own inline frontmatter parser that was NOT updated to handle JSON-encoded values. It still runs the AAR-0005-era quote-strip logic:

```typescript
// sync.ts:709
if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
  val = val.slice(1, -1);
}
```

**Concrete corruption paths for any staged file with special characters:**

| Input field (via writeStagedProposal) | On-disk source line | disk parseFm (new, correct) | sync.ts parse (old, bug) | brain.db stored |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Title with "quotes"` | `title: "Title with \"quotes\""` | `Title with "quotes"` | `Title with \"quotes\"` | CORRUPT |
| `Line 1\nLine 2` (real newline) | `reason: "Line 1\nLine 2"` (literal `\n`) | `Line 1` + LF + `Line 2` | `Line 1\nLine 2` (literal backslash-n) | CORRUPT |
| `path\to\thing` (backslashes) | `section: "path\\to\\thing"` | `path\to\thing` | `path\\to\\thing` | CORRUPT |
| Simple ASCII with no metachars | `title: "Simple title"` | `Simple title` | `Simple title` | OK (lucky) |

**Impact:**
- Morning briefing (`morning.ts → getPendingProposals → listStagedProposals`) reads disk via the upgraded parseFm. Displays correctly.
- `brain --search-staged` (`brain.ts:702 → queries.ts:searchStaged → staged_proposals table`) reads brain.db which was populated by sync.ts's legacy parser. Displays corrupt values.
- `brain --recall-all` (same path). Displays corrupt values.

**Practical risk today:** Low. All observed Gemini outputs are single-line ASCII with no embedded quotes in `title` or `reason`. Existing enum-like values for `section` and `reason` don't trigger the bug. The round-trip test passes because it only exercises the disk path (`writeStagedProposal` → `listStagedProposals`), which both go through the new parseFm.

**Latent risk (matches the exact threat Finding 3 was meant to neutralize):** The moment AAR-0009 or any downstream change introduces a multi-line `reason` or a title with embedded quotes, brain.db silently stores corrupted values while disk stays clean. The two views of the same data diverge. Reviewers using `brain --search-staged` see escape artifacts; the morning briefing doesn't. Hard to debug, trivial to avoid by fixing sync.ts now.

**This is bad intelligence in my own task doc.** The "Out of Scope" section explicitly said:

> Touching `syncStaged()` in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`. The orphan-removal pass already handles archived files correctly because the `staged/` scan is non-recursive and skips subdirectories.

The out-of-scope entry addressed orphan-removal but silently assumed sync.ts's frontmatter parser was the same code path as staged.ts's parseFm. It isn't — sync.ts has its own copy, and it wasn't updated. This is a duplicated-logic hazard I failed to notice when writing the doc. Ryan followed the doc faithfully; the gap is mine.

---

### Required fix

Sync.ts lines 700-714 need the same parser upgrade that landed in staged.ts:

- Detect leading `"` → try JSON.parse → fall back to legacy strip
- OR (cleaner) refactor to call `parseFm` from `staged.ts` directly. Currently `parseFm` is module-private. Either export it or mirror the logic.

**Recommendation:** Export `parseFm` from `staged.ts` as `parseStagedFrontmatter` and import it in `sync.ts`. Single source of truth. Prevents this exact bug from recurring.

**Scope of the fix:** 1 file, ~10 lines changed. Still bundles into the AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B single commit.

---

### Cross-cutting checks

1. **Other frontmatter parsers:** `src/libs/frontmatter.ts` (`parseFrontmatter`) is used by the broader sync pipeline for memories/references/projects but NOT for staged proposals. Those files still use YAML-ish formats written by humans, not the JSON-encoded writer. Not affected.
2. **Backwards compatibility (new reader + old files):** Verified. Legacy files with single-quoted values hit the `else if (val.startsWith("'"))` branch. Legacy files with double-quoted simple values (no escapes) pass through JSON.parse cleanly because `"simple title"` IS valid JSON.
3. **Forwards compatibility (old reader + new files):** THIS is the sync.ts hole. Any future parser that copies the legacy pattern will break on JSON-encoded values.
4. **`bun build` typecheck:** Clean for all four touched files. No type errors.
5. **`bun test staged-roundtrip.test.ts`:** 2 pass, 0 fail, 12 expect() calls. Runs cleanly on Windows despite the known segfault issue — no import-from-modified-modules pattern triggered.

---

### Regression risks Ryan didn't catch

Ryan can't be faulted for missing the sync.ts gap because:
1. The task doc explicitly marked sync.ts out of scope for this task.
2. The failure path is latent today — current data doesn't exercise it.
3. Ryan's own tests pass because they only exercise the disk path, which is what the task doc told him to test.

Nothing else flagged. Implementation is otherwise tight.

---

### Single-commit bundle readiness

NOT READY. Fix sync.ts first, then bundle AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B + the sync.ts fix into one commit. Commit message should reference all three: `AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B: staged content bug fix + review findings + sync parser upgrade` (or similar).

Rationale for bundling the sync.ts fix into the same commit rather than a new task:
- It's ~10 lines in one file.

- Shipping AAR-0008B without it leaves a latent corruption path that undermines the whole point of Finding 3.
- A separate AAR-0008C would mean three task IDs for what is functionally one cleanup pass.

Small addendum to AAR-0008B — fix a duplicate frontmatter parser in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts` that McCall caught in the code review. This bundles into the same single commit as AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B, no new task doc.

## The problem

`src/libs/brain/sync.ts` lines 700-714 has its OWN inline frontmatter parser for staged proposal files:

```typescript
const fmMatch = rawContent.match(/^---\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---/);
const fm: Record<string, string> = {};
if (fmMatch) {
  for (const line of fmMatch[1].split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const colonIdx = line.indexOf(":");
    if (colonIdx < 0) continue;
    const key = line.slice(0, colonIdx).trim();
    let val = line.slice(colonIdx + 1).trim();
    if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
      val = val.slice(1, -1);
    }
    if (key) fm[key] = val;
  }
}
```

This is a COPY of the old `parseFm` logic from `staged.ts` before AAR-0008B upgraded the staged.ts version to handle JSON-encoded values. Because sync.ts has its own copy, the AAR-0008B upgrade didn't reach it.

Result: files written by the new `fmEscape()` (JSON.stringify) are read correctly by staged.ts's new parseFm but incorrectly by sync.ts's old parser. The on-disk view (morning briefing) and the brain.db view (`brain --search-staged`, `brain --recall-all`) will diverge silently for any title or reason containing double quotes, newlines, or backslashes.

## The fix

**Export `parseFm` from `src/libs/staged.ts`** under a more specific name so it's clearly the canonical staged-frontmatter parser, and **import it in `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`** to replace the inline parser.

### File 1: `src/libs/staged.ts`

1. Export the existing `parseFm` function. Rename it to `parseStagedFrontmatter` for clarity (it's the canonical staged-file parser, and the new name makes it obvious to future readers that other files shouldn't copy-paste it).
2. Update the internal call site in `listStagedProposals` to use the new name.
3. All behavior unchanged — this is purely a rename + export.

### File 2: `src/libs/brain/sync.ts`

1. Import `parseStagedFrontmatter` from `../staged.js`.
2. Replace the inline parser block (lines 700-714) with a single call to `parseStagedFrontmatter(rawContent)`.
3. The rest of the `syncStaged` function stays the same — it still reads `fm.title`, `fm.section`, `fm.change_type`, etc.

### Verification

1. Run `bun test src/libs/__tests__/staged-roundtrip.test.ts` — both existing tests must still pass.
2. Run `bun run tool brain --check` — should sync cleanly without errors. If any existing staged files are on disk, they should sync to brain.db without changes.
3. Manually construct a staged file with tricky characters (embedded quotes, newlines) in title or reason using the synthetic test agent, then run `brain --check` and `brain --search-staged` to verify brain.db matches disk. If you can't easily do this end-to-end manually, document what you DID verify and what you couldn't.
4. Grep the codebase for any OTHER copies of the staged frontmatter parser that might need updating: `grep -n "^---" src/libs src/tools` and look for any regex doing `match[1].split(/\r?\n/)` style parsing on staged files.

## Constraints

- Sequential. No parallel edits.
- 2 files max: `staged.ts` (rename + export) and `sync.ts` (import + delete inline parser).
- Do NOT change the behavior of `parseFm` itself — the rename and export are purely refactoring.
- Do NOT commit. Freddie commits the single bundled commit after this addendum lands.
- Do NOT touch AAR-0009 code or any unrelated files.
- This is a ~15 line change. Keep it minimal.

## What to report

- Git diff summary (should be 2 files, small)
- Test results
- Whether brain --check ran cleanly
- Any other parser copies found via grep
- Any deviations and why
- Confirmation that AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B state is preserved

Do NOT commit. Stop when done and wait for Freddie to merge.

content.text:
## Bundle ready to commit

**9 files modified + 2 new files (test + migration tool).** 104 lines added, 36 removed across 9 files.

```
src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts  |   3 ++-    (the 1-line bug fix + title field)
src/libs/staged.ts               |  49 +++++  (title field, fmEscape, parseStagedFrontmatter export)
src/libs/brain/schema.ts         |   1 +     (title column)
src/libs/brain/index.ts          |  28 ++++  (idempotent ALTER + FTS5 rebuild + trigger drop)
src/libs/brain/fts.ts            |   4 +-    (staged_fts title column + triggers)
src/libs/brain/sync.ts           |  27 --    (deleted inline parser, use parseStagedFrontmatter)
src/libs/brain/queries.ts        |   4 +-    (title in StagedResult)
src/tools/brain.ts               |  20 +++   (formatStagedSnippet + title display)
src/tools/morning.ts             |   4 +-    (title-first preview)
src/libs/__tests__/staged-roundtrip.test.ts   (new, 2 tests pass)
src/tools/migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts            (new, filters by title absence)
```

## What lands in this single commit

- **AAR-0008:** Bug fix at `autonomous-distill.ts:625` (content instead of title), `StagedProposal.title` as first-class field, schema/FTS5 migration, round-trip test, migration tool
- **AAR-0008B Finding 1:** Migration tool filters by `title:` absence in frontmatter (idempotent, safe to re-run)
- **AAR-0008B Finding 2:** `brain --search-staged` and `--recall-all` CLI display shows title as primary label
- **AAR-0008B Finding 3:** Option B — `fmEscape()` via JSON.stringify + upgraded `parseStagedFrontmatter` with JSON decode and legacy fallback
- **Sync.ts parser consolidation:** Eliminated duplicate frontmatter parser in `sync.ts`, now imports canonical `parseStagedFrontmatter` from `staged.ts`. Single source of truth prevents recurrence of the duplicate-parser class of bug.

Plus task docs at `vault/studio/projects/autonomous-agent-research/tasks/AAR-0008*.md` and updated `TASKS.md`.

## Ready to commit?

If yes, commit message will read:

```
AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B: staged proposal content bug + review findings

Fix _stageProposal() in autonomous-distill.ts writing proposal.title
instead of proposal.content, which wiped the body of every staged file
written by the AAR Phase 1 autonomous distill pipeline.

- Add title as a first-class field on StagedProposal
- Add title column to brain.db staged_proposals (idempotent ALTER +
  FTS5 rebuild with trigger drop)
- Upgrade frontmatter escape via JSON.stringify + parseStagedFrontmatter
  with legacy fallback for pre-existing files
- Consolidate duplicate frontmatter parser in sync.ts — single source
  of truth in staged.ts (parseStagedFrontmatter)
- Surface title in brain --search-staged / --recall-all CLI display
- Migration tool migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts filters by title absence,
  safe to re-run

The 10 auto-applied soul changes in fce3a2f are unaffected — applySoul-
Change correctly used proposal.content. Only the staged path was broken.
The 77 corrupted staged files on disk cannot be recovered; user runs
migrate-staged-pre-fix --apply manually after this commit to archive
them, then re-runs the pipeline to regenerate clean proposals.

Refs AAR-0008, AAR-0008B
```

After the commit lands, sequence continues:
1. `bun run tool migrate-staged-pre-fix --dry-run` → confirm 77
2. `bun run tool migrate-staged-pre-fix --apply` → archive
3. `bun run tool brain --check` → orphan cleanup
4. Dispatch Ryan for AAR-0009 (optimization)
5. McCall reviews AAR-0009
6. Second commit for AAR-0009
7. Re-run pipeline with content-preserving + optimized code
8. Triage the new staged proposals

**Proceed with commit?**

#!/usr/bin/env bun
/**
 * brain.ts — SQLite + FTS5 brain for memory/reference lookup and decision tracking
 *
 * Dual-mode: CLI (`bun src/tools/brain.ts --sync`) or native tool via registry.
 * Provides fast full-text search across vault memory and reference files,
 * plus decision logging with outcome tracking.
 */

import { z } from "zod";
import type { ToolDefinition } from "../registry/types.js";
import {
  initBrain,
  shutdownBrain,
  syncMemories,
  syncReferences,
  checkSync,
  searchMemories,
  searchReferences,
  searchDecisions,
  logDecision,
  updateOutcome,
  getDecisionHistory,
  getStats,
  setMemoryEmbedding,
  hybridSearchMemories,
  upsertEntity,
  addEntityRelation,
  getEntity,
  searchEntities,

  getEntityStats,

  syncProjects,

  embedUnembeddedMemories,
  embedUnembeddedDecisions,
  hybridSearchChunks,
  hybridSearchDecisions,
  searchAll,
  hybridSearchRaw,
  searchRecall,
  searchStaged,
} from "../libs/brain/index.js";
import { embedText, EMBEDDING_CONFIG } from "../libs/embeddings.js";
import { isEmbeddingStale } from "../libs/brain/degradation.js";

// ── Display helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────

content.text:
Found it. Ryan's AAR-0008 migration drops and recreates `staged_fts` but **never rebuilds the FTS5 index** from existing `staged_proposals` rows. When the AFTER DELETE trigger fires, FTS5 tries to look up the old row in an empty index and throws "malformed" — even though `integrity_check` passes because the tables themselves are structurally valid.

Testing the theory by running the FTS5 rebuild command directly:

input.command: bun -e "import { getRawDb } from './src/libs/sqlite.js'; import { initBrain } from './src/libs/brain/index.js'; initBrain(); const db = getRawDb(); try { db.exec(\"INSERT INTO staged_fts(staged_fts) VALUES('rebuild')\"); console.log('rebuild OK'); const count = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM staged_fts').get(); console.log('rows in staged_fts:', count); const tc = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM staged_proposals').get(); console.log('rows in staged_proposals:', tc); } catch (e) { console.error('rebuild failed:', e.message); }" 2>&1

lines: // staged_fts must be rebuilt to include title (AAR-0008)
// FTS5 virtual tables can't have columns added at runtime — drop and recreate
.prepare("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='staged_fts'")
-      // initFts() below will recreate staged_fts with the new column set
+      // initFts() below will recreate staged_fts with the new column set.
+      // We must then rebuild the FTS index from existing staged_proposals
+      // rows — without this, the new index is empty while the content table
+      // has rows, and AFTER DELETE triggers throw "database disk image is
+      // malformed" because FTS5 can't find the old rowid in the index.
// Create FTS5 virtual tables and triggers
+  // Rebuild staged_fts index from existing staged_proposals rows if we just
+  // recreated the virtual table. Idempotent but only necessary after migration.
+      console.error("[brain] staged_fts rebuild failed:", (err as Error).message);
// Load sqlite-vec extension and create vector virtual tables

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix: rebuild staged_fts index after AAR-0008 migration

After dropping and recreating staged_fts to add the title column, the
new virtual table is empty while staged_proposals still has rows.
AFTER DELETE triggers then throw "database disk image is malformed"
because FTS5 can't find the old rowid in the index — a confusing error
since PRAGMA integrity_check passes.

Add INSERT INTO staged_fts(staged_fts) VALUES('rebuild') right after
initFts() populates the recreated index from the content table. Gated
by stagedFtsNeedsRebuild so it only runs on the migration path, not
every startup.

Caught by running migrate-staged-pre-fix --apply followed by
brain --check on a DB where the 77 staged rows already existed. Tests
unchanged — the bug only manifests with pre-existing rows, which the
round-trip test doesn't create before the migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
## Status after this detour

| Commit | Content |
|---|---|
| `9eeaca7` | AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B: content bug + review findings |
| `a3c327f` | FTS5 rebuild fix (caught running the migration) |

**Migration complete:**
- 77 corrupted files archived to `vault/studio/memory/{agent}/staged/archive-pre-fix/`
- brain.db `staged_proposals` + `staged_fts` both at 0 rows (clean)
- Round-trip tests pass (2/2)
- `brain --check` clean

**Lesson from the rebuild bug:** McCall caught Ryan's trigger-drop omission in the AAR-0008 review but missed that the NEW table also needs `rebuild` to populate from the existing content table. This is a second-order FTS5-external-content gotcha that only surfaces with live data + orphan removal — the round-trip test didn't catch it because it creates files fresh instead of exercising a pre-populated DB through migration. Worth noting for AAR-0009's benchmark test: it should include a "DB with pre-existing rows" scenario.

## Ready for AAR-0009

The AAR-0009 task doc already exists at `vault/studio/projects/autonomous-agent-research/tasks/2026-04-07-151137-AAR-0009-distill-pipeline-optimization.md` from McCall's earlier pass. It covers:
- Distill cache per knowledge-hash
- Two-phase gate ordering (cheap first, short-circuit)
- Per-agent rule embedding cache (in-memory per run)
- Batched contradiction check (1 Gemini call per proposal instead of N)
- Mandatory benchmark asserting hot run < 30s

**Dispatch Ryan sequentially for AAR-0009?** Or do you want to review the task doc first, or make any adjustments (e.g., add a "DB with pre-existing rows" scenario to the benchmark to catch bugs like the one we just hit)?

content.content: Updated task #5 status

input.prompt:
Implement AAR-0009 — pipeline optimization phase 1 for the autonomous distill pipeline. The task doc is at:

`vault/studio/projects/autonomous-agent-research/tasks/2026-04-07-151137-AAR-0009-distill-pipeline-optimization.md`

Read it end-to-end before writing code. McCall wrote it with file-by-file specs, function signatures, an ADR section, and 18 acceptance criteria. Don't deviate from the scope it defines.

## Critical context

1. **Builds on committed AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B.** Two commits are already on main:
   - `9eeaca7` — AAR-0008 + AAR-0008B bundle (content bug fix + review findings)
   - `a3c327f` — FTS5 rebuild fix after migration
   
   The `StagedProposal` type now has `title: string` and `proposedChange: string` (content). `_stageProposal()` correctly passes both. `parseStagedFrontmatter` is the canonical frontmatter parser in `staged.ts` — use it if you need to read staged files.

2. **Pipeline is currently clean.** 77 corrupted staged files were migrated to archive-pre-fix/. `brain.db` staged tables are at 0 rows. Any pipeline work you do won't conflict with leftover data.

3. **Sequential only. No parallel worktrees.** User explicitly wants one thing at a time to prevent merge conflicts and allow monitoring.

4. **Scope — four things the task doc covers:**
   - **Distill cache** — new lib `src/libs/distill-cache.ts`, file-system storage at `vault/studio/usage/distill-cache/`, key = sha256(soul + agent knowledge dir + shared knowledge dir + `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION`). Defensive reads with structural validation. `--no-cache` CLI flag on `distill-soul` and `autonomous-distill` to force bypass.
   - **Two-phase gate ordering** — split `runAllGates()` into phase 1 (safety/size/drift, parallel, deterministic) and phase 2 (regression/constitution, parallel, LLM). Short-circuit on phase 1 failure with synthetic "skipped — phase 1 failure" GateResults so the `results[]` array always has 5 entries. `failedGates` only lists actual failures, not skipped ones.
   - **Per-run rule embedding cache** — `RuleEmbeddingCache` interface, in-memory Map keyed by sha256 of soul content. Invalidates automatically when soul mutates after auto-apply. Lifetime = single pipeline run.
   - **Batched contradiction check** — replace the O(rules × changes) per-pair Gemini calls in `runRegressionGate()` with a single Gemini call evaluating all high-similarity pairs at once. Regex-parse the response. Cap at top-20 by similarity. Fallback to per-pair `sim > 0.92` treatment on parse or Gemini failure.

5. **Benchmark is mandatory.** `src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts` runs cold + hot, asserts hot < 30000ms or exits non-zero. Read the task doc carefully for the exact structure.

## Lesson from the rebuild bug — add one scenario to the benchmark

input.prompt:
Code review of AAR-0009 — pipeline optimization phase 1. The task doc is at:

`vault/studio/projects/autonomous-agent-research/tasks/2026-04-07-151137-AAR-0009-distill-pipeline-optimization.md`

You wrote it. Verify Ryan implemented it correctly.

## Ryan's self-report summary

**Files:**
- NEW: `src/libs/distill-cache.ts` — file-system cache with deterministic key, defensive reads
- MODIFIED: `src/libs/gates.ts` — RuleEmbeddingCache interface, two-phase runAllGates, batched contradiction check in runRegressionGate
- MODIFIED: `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` — AutonomousDistillOptions, cache wiring around callDistillGemini, ruleCache threaded into GateInput
- MODIFIED: `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts` — --no-cache CLI flag and tool schema field
- MODIFIED: `src/tools/distill-soul.ts` — comment reminder to bump DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION
- NEW: `src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts` — cold/hot + pre-populated DB scenario

**AC status:** 14 PASS, 3 NOT RUN (15/16/17 — benchmark execution, deferred because Ryan read my instructions as "do not run pipeline"), 1 code-audit verification (AC 2-3).

**Deviations Ryan flagged:**
1. Static ESM import for `fs` in `distill-cache.ts` instead of my `require("fs")` from the task doc (anti-pattern fix, no behavior change).
2. Removed unused `embedText` import from `gates.ts`.
3. Pre-populated DB scenario inserts rows via raw SQL, not `writeStagedProposal`.
4. AC 2-3 verified by code audit, not live soul mutation.

## Review dimensions

### 1. Distill cache correctness

Read `src/libs/distill-cache.ts`. Verify:
- `buildCacheKey(agent: string): string` hashes soul file content + agent knowledge dir contents + shared knowledge dir contents + DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION
- Key is deterministic: same inputs → same key
- Key is sensitive: any file content change → different key
- Key includes `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION` from distill-soul.ts — cache invalidates when the prompt changes
- `readDistillCache(key)` returns null on: file missing, JSON parse error, version mismatch, or structural validation failure
- `writeDistillCache(key, value)` creates parent dir idempotently
- `clearDistillCache()` removes all cache files (any `--clear-cache` flag, or just the function exists for use)
- File format is JSON with fields: version, timestamp, rawOutput, inputTokens, outputTokens, cachedAt (or similar — verify matches task doc spec)
- No path traversal risk: the key is a hex hash, never user-controlled directly
- Cache dir: `vault/studio/usage/distill-cache/` per task doc

### 2. Distill cache wiring

Read `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` and `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts`. Verify:
- Cache read before `callDistillGemini` in the per-agent loop
- Cache hit: skip Gemini call, use cached rawOutput, log stderr "cache HIT" or equivalent
- Cache miss: call Gemini as before, write result to cache on success
- `--no-cache` flag on CLI bypasses read but still writes (or bypasses both — verify task doc intent)
- `--no-cache` flag is plumbed through to `autonomousDistill(agents, options)`
- Errors in cache read do NOT crash the pipeline — they fall back to Gemini call
- Errors in cache write do NOT crash the pipeline — they log and continue

### 3. Two-phase gate ordering

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `runAllGates()`. Verify:
- Phase 1: safety, size, drift run in parallel via `Promise.allSettled`
- Phase 1 failure short-circuits: phase 2 does NOT run, regression + constitution get synthetic "skipped — phase 1 failure" GateResults
- Phase 1 success: phase 2 runs (regression + constitution in parallel)
- Return value `results[]` always has 5 entries in order: safety, size, drift, regression, constitution
- Return value `failedGates[]` only includes actual failures, NOT skipped gates
- Return value `passed` is false if any actual failure, true only if all 5 actually ran and passed
- The existing AAR-0003 property "all 5 gates always run on success" is preserved
- The new behavior "expensive gates skipped on phase 1 failure" is only applied on the failure path

### 4. Rule embedding cache

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `RuleEmbeddingCache`. Verify:
- Interface has get/set semantics keyed by soul content hash (not by agent name — agent identity alone can't detect soul mutation mid-run)
- `createRuleEmbeddingCache()` factory exists
- Cache is per-run (not module-level global) — verify it's created fresh in `autonomousDistill()` and passed via `GateInput.ruleEmbeddingCache`
- `runRegressionGate()` checks cache before calling `embedBatch(rules)` — cache hit skips the embed call
- Cache key is sha256 of soul content — mutations invalidate automatically
- No stale embedding reuse across different soul versions

### 5. Batched contradiction check

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `runRegressionGate()` and any new helper like `batchedContradictionCheck`. Verify:
- Collects all `(rule, change, similarity)` tuples where similarity > REGRESSION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
- Caps at top 20 by similarity (highest first)
- Makes ONE Gemini call with all pairs in a single prompt
- Parses response with a regex that extracts per-pair verdicts
- Fallback on parse failure: per-pair `sim > 0.92` treated as contradiction
- Fallback on Gemini API failure: same per-pair fallback
- Caller name is `gate-regression-batched` or similar (for ai-usage tracking)
- Response format: ask Gemini to emit one line per pair with PAIR-{n}: CONTRADICTION|COMPATIBLE or similar
- Edge cases: 0 pairs → skip Gemini entirely; 1 pair → still use batched format for consistency; 20+ pairs → top 20 only

### 6. Benchmark structure

Read `src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts`. Verify:
- Cold scenario: cache empty, full pipeline runs
- Hot scenario: cache populated from cold run, pipeline runs again
- Hot scenario asserts `durationMs < 30000` or process.exit(1)
- Both scenarios write timing output (console or file)
- Pre-populated DB scenario: creates synthetic rows in `staged_proposals`, runs pipeline or syncStaged orphan-removal path, asserts no SQLite errors, cleans up in `finally`
- Scenarios are independent — can run in any order
- Cleanup is robust: `finally` blocks remove synthetic test agent dirs and DB rows
- File uses `__bench_prepop__` or similar synthetic agent name (doesn't collide with real agents)

### 7. AAR-0008 / AAR-0008B preservation

Grep and spot-check:
- `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts:625-626` still has the bug fix (title + proposedChange=content)
- `src/libs/staged.ts` still has parseStagedFrontmatter, fmEscape, title field
- `src/libs/brain/sync.ts` still imports parseStagedFrontmatter
- `src/libs/brain/index.ts` still has the FTS5 rebuild fix (stagedFtsNeedsRebuild + INSERT rebuild)
- Round-trip test `src/libs/__tests__/staged-roundtrip.test.ts` not modified
- Migration tool `src/tools/migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts` not modified

### 8. Correctness under optimization

This is the critical one. The optimizations MUST NOT change correctness. For each optimization:

- **Distill cache:** Cached rawOutput must be byte-identical to what Gemini would return on re-invocation IF inputs are unchanged. Verify the cache key covers EVERY input that affects Gemini output: soul content, knowledge file contents, the prompt template. Missing any one = silent stale proposal.
- **Two-phase gates:** Skipped gates must not be treated as passing. `passed` must be false. Callers of `runAllGates` must handle "skipped" gates in their result display (check morning.ts and any staged display — they might render "skipped — phase 1 failure" as a spurious failure text).
- **Rule embedding cache:** A per-run in-memory Map must not leak across agents within the same run. If agent tala and agent rune have different soul files but the cache is keyed by soul hash (not agent), they get different cache entries automatically. But verify: is the cache passed per-agent (new cache per agent) or per-run (shared across agents)? If shared, the soul-hash key handles it. If per-agent, verify it's created in the right scope.
- **Batched contradictions:** The single Gemini call must ask about THE SAME pairs the per-pair version would have. If the batched prompt drops pairs or reorders them, silent skips. Also: the response parser must tolerate partial responses (Gemini returning fewer lines than expected).

### 9. Task doc gaps / bad intelligence

- Did you spec anywhere in AAR-0009 that Ryan should have run the benchmark? If yes, Ryan misread your instructions. If no, update the ADR to reflect that benchmark execution is the verification step, not the implementation step — the operator (Freddie) runs it post-merge.
- Any file paths or function signatures Ryan had to guess because the task doc was vague?
- Any edge cases the task doc missed that Ryan handled via judgment?

### 10. Cross-cutting

- `bun build` typecheck clean on all changed files
- Any circular import between gates.ts and distill-cache.ts?
- Any new dependencies added to package.json? There shouldn't be — only built-ins.
- The `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION` constant: where is it defined? Exported? Imported by distill-cache.ts? Verify the integration point.

## What to report

- **Verdict:** APPROVED / BLOCK / CHANGES REQUESTED
- Per-dimension findings with file:line references
- Verdict on Ryan's 4 flagged deviations (accept/reject each)
- Any bad intelligence in your own AAR-0009 task doc
- Any correctness risks you want to flag before the benchmark runs
- Overall risk assessment
- Whether you think Freddie should run the benchmark before committing, OR commit first and run benchmark in a follow-up

Do NOT modify files. Do NOT touch any other code. Review only.

content.text:
No dependency changes. Clean. I have everything I need to render the verdict.

---

# AAR-0009 Code Review — McCall

**Verdict: APPROVED with notes** (2 small follow-ups, no blockers)

The implementation is faithful to the task doc, defensive in all the right places, and Ryan made three good judgment calls that I want to keep. One thing I want to flag for the operator before benchmark, and one bad-intelligence item in the task doc that I'll own.

---

## Per-dimension findings

### 1. Distill cache correctness — PASS
`src/libs/distill-cache.ts:44-57` — `buildCacheKey()` composes soul + agent knowledge dir + shared knowledge dir + `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION`, sorts filenames before hashing (`distill-cache.ts:66`), returns 16-char hex slice. Deterministic. Sensitive to any input change.

`distill-cache.ts:64-74` — `hashDirectoryContents` returns `"empty"` sentinel when dir missing or no .md files, hashes filename + content sorted. Stable across Windows/Linux.

`distill-cache.ts:82-104` — `readDistillCache` validates: file exists, JSON parses, entry is object, version matches, agent matches, key matches, result.rawOutput is string, **and** `result.noChanges` is boolean. The last check is stronger than my spec — Ryan added it as defensive validation. Accept.

`distill-cache.ts:110-128` — `writeDistillCache` calls `mkdirSync({ recursive: true })`, swallows all throws. Non-fatal. Correct.

`distill-cache.ts:135-149` — `clearDistillCache` exists, removes all .json files, returns count. No path traversal: cache key is sha256 hex slice, never user-controlled.

Cache dir matches spec: `vault/studio/usage/distill-cache/`.

### 2. Distill cache wiring — PASS
`src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts:413-426` — cache read happens before `callDistillGemini`. Cache hit logs "cache HIT", uses cached result. Cache miss calls Gemini, writes result, logs "cache MISS". Both branches stderr-log with the cache key for observability.

`autonomous-distill.ts:382` — `noCache = opts?.noCache ?? false`. `autonomous-distill.ts:418` — `noCache ? null : readDistillCache(...)` correctly bypasses **read** but still **writes** on the live call (line 424 always runs after a Gemini call). This is the more useful semantics — `--no-cache` becomes a "force refresh", not a "disable caching entirely". Matches my ADR-3 escape-hatch intent.

`src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts:98-100, 105, 157-158, 163` — `--no-cache` flag plumbed through both CLI and tool registry paths. Schema description matches behavior.

Cache read errors return null (caught by the try/catch in `readDistillCache`), so the `cached` variable becomes null and the pipeline falls through to the live Gemini call. Cache write errors are swallowed inside `writeDistillCache`. Pipeline never crashes on cache faults. Correct.

### 3. Two-phase gate ordering — PASS
`src/libs/gates.ts:622-674` — Phase 1 (`safety`, `size`, `drift`) via `Promise.allSettled`. Phase 1 short-circuit returns 5 entries with synthetic skipped results for `regression` and `constitution` (`gates.ts:638-644`). `failedGates` filters phase 1 results only on the short-circuit branch (`gates.ts:646-648`) — does NOT include skipped phase 2 gates. Matches AC 9.

On the success path (`gates.ts:653-674`), all 5 gates run, `failedGates` is computed from the full results array, and `passed = failedGates.length === 0`. The AAR-0003 property "all 5 gates always run on success" is preserved — short-circuit only fires on the failure path.

`unwrap()` (`gates.ts:581-588`) handles a `Promise.allSettled` rejection by synthesizing a `pass: false` result with the rejection message. Defensive.

### 4. Rule embedding cache — PASS
`gates.ts:50-62` — `RuleEmbeddingCache` interface and `createRuleEmbeddingCache()` factory. Cache uses `Map<string, CachedRuleEmbeddings>`, keyed by soul **content hash** (not agent name). Created per-run.

`autonomous-distill.ts:393` — `const ruleCache: RuleEmbeddingCache = createRuleEmbeddingCache();` is created once at the top of `autonomousDistill()`, **before** the agent loop. **Shared across all agents and proposals.** This is correct because the key is the soul content hash — different agents have different soul content, so they get different cache entries automatically. Auto-applies that mutate a soul invalidate via the new hash. Spec-aligned.

`autonomous-distill.ts:498` — `ruleEmbeddingCache: ruleCache` threaded into `GateInput` for every proposal.

`gates.ts:413-442` — `runRegressionGate` computes `soulHash = sha256(currentSoul)`, checks `input.ruleEmbeddingCache?.get(soulHash)`. Cache hit reuses rules + embeddings. Cache miss extracts rules, embeds via `embedBatch(rules, "gate-regression")`, stores in cache. The optional-chaining (`?.`) means the cache is genuinely opt-in: callers that don't pass one still work, they just don't get the savings.

### 5. Batched contradiction check — PASS
`gates.ts:341-393` — `batchedContradictionCheck()`. Builds prompt with one numbered pair per `Pair N:` block. Calls Gemini with `caller: "gate-regression-batched"` (matches spec). Parses with regex `^${i+1}\\.\\s*(CONTRADICTION|COMPATIBLE)` per pair. Missing line falls back to per-pair `sim > 0.92` heuristic. Whole-call failure (catch block) returns full per-pair fallback. Both fallback paths match the spec.

Edge cases in `runRegressionGate`:
- Zero candidates → returns early without calling Gemini (`gates.ts:480-482`).
- 1 pair → still uses batched format (consistency).
- 20+ pairs → top 20 by similarity after `candidates.sort((a,b) => b.sim - a.sim).slice(0, 20)` (`gates.ts:485-486`). Comment documents the cap.

Edge case I want to flag (not a bug): if Gemini returns the wrong number of lines but **none** of the missing pairs have `sim > 0.92`, the fallback marks them all COMPATIBLE silently. That's the same behavior the per-pair version had, and it's documented. Not a regression. Acceptable.

### 6. Benchmark structure — PASS, but see note in §10
`src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts` has all three scenarios:

- **Pre-populated DB scenario** (`bench.ts:30-138`): runs **first**, before the cache benchmark. Inserts 3 synthetic rows for `__bench_prepop__`, runs `syncStaged()` orphan removal, asserts row count goes to 0, catches FTS5 errors. `finally` block at line 129-138 cleans up `staged_proposals` and `sync_meta` rows for the synthetic agent. Robust.
- **Cold scenario** (`bench.ts:142-154`): clears cache, runs pipeline, captures `cold` ms.
- **Hot scenario** (`bench.ts:165-169`): runs pipeline again, captures `hot` ms. Asserts `hot < TARGET_MS` at line 194, exits 1 on failure.

Independence: pre-populated runs first and cleans up before cache benchmark begins. Cache benchmark uses real agent `echo` (default), not the synthetic `__bench_prepop__`, so no collision.

One stylistic note (not a finding): the SQL `INSERT` uses string interpolation rather than parameter binding (`bench.ts:84-94`). The synthetic agent name and rows are hardcoded constants, so there's no injection risk in this specific file, and Ryan correctly uses `.replace(/'/g, "''")` for fields with apostrophes. Acceptable for a benchmark scaffold. **Don't generalize this pattern to production code paths.**

### 7. AAR-0008 / AAR-0008B preservation — PASS
- `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts:657-658` — `title: proposal.title, proposedChange: proposal.content` with the inline `// FIXED: was proposal.title (AAR-0008)` comment intact.
- `src/libs/staged.ts:47, 67` — `fmEscape` and `parseStagedFrontmatter` present.
- `src/libs/brain/sync.ts:16, 702` — `parseStagedFrontmatter` import + usage.
- `src/libs/brain/index.ts:295-326` — `stagedFtsNeedsRebuild` flag + `INSERT INTO staged_fts(staged_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` rebuild path intact.
- `git status --short` confirms `src/libs/__tests__/staged-roundtrip.test.ts` and `src/tools/migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts` are not in the modified set.

### 8. Correctness under optimization — PASS
- **Distill cache key:** covers soul content + agent knowledge dir + shared knowledge dir + prompt version. **Missing input I want to flag:** the cache key does NOT include the `loadDir()` formatting prefix (`### {filename}\n`) from `distill-soul.ts:43`. If someone changes `loadDir()` to format files differently — say, switches `### ` to `## ` — the prompt sent to Gemini will change but the cache key will not. This is structurally identical to "the prompt template changed", so the existing rule "bump `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION` when prompt changes" covers it. I'll add a follow-up note to the prompt-version comment to make this explicit. **Not a blocker.**
- **Two-phase gates:** Skipped phase 2 gates have `pass: false` and a "skipped" reason. `formatGateResults()` (`staged.ts:96-106`) renders them as a row in the gate table — readable to humans, no spurious "failure" in `failedGates`. `judge-panel.ts:49` consumes `gateResults.results` for the prompt — judges only run when phase 1 passed, so they never see the synthetic skipped entries. Safe.
- **Rule embedding cache:** Per-run scope, soul-hash key. Cross-agent reuse is impossible because soul files differ. Same-agent within-run mutation invalidates via hash. No leak.
- **Batched contradictions:** `pairs` is always the same `top` array used for `contradictions[]` extraction (`gates.ts:486, 489, 492`). Indices line up. No silent drops.

### 9. Task doc gaps / bad intelligence — 1 finding (mine)

**Bad intelligence in AAR-0009:** the task doc spec for `distill-cache.ts` at line 365 used `const { rmSync } = require("fs");` inside `clearDistillCache()`. Ryan correctly flagged this as a TS/ESM anti-pattern and lifted the import to the top of the file. **Accept the deviation** — Ryan was right. I'll fix the task doc copy in a follow-up.

Otherwise, every file path, function signature, and edge case Ryan needed was in the task doc. No guessing.

### 10. Cross-cutting — PASS
- `bun build --target=bun --outdir=out/typecheck-aar0009 ...` bundles all 6 files (389 modules) cleanly. No type errors. No missing imports.
- No circular import: `gates.ts` does not import `distill-cache.ts`; `distill-cache.ts` does not import `gates.ts`. They're connected only through `autonomous-distill.ts`.
- `git diff package.json bun.lock` empty. No new dependencies. Cache uses `crypto`, `fs`, `path` — all built-ins.
- `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION` is exported from `distill-cache.ts:29` and referenced **only** by the cache module itself + the comment at `distill-soul.ts:96-97`. The comment correctly tells future editors where to bump. Integration is correct.

---

## Verdict on Ryan's 4 flagged deviations

| # | Deviation | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Static ESM `import { rmSync }` in `distill-cache.ts` instead of `require("fs")` from task doc | **ACCEPT.** Bad intelligence in my task doc. Ryan was right. |
| 2 | Removed unused `embedText` import from `gates.ts` | **ACCEPT.** Dead code removal. Confirmed with `Grep` — no `embedText` reference remains. |
| 3 | Pre-populated DB scenario inserts via raw SQL, not `writeStagedProposal` | **ACCEPT.** The whole point of the pre-populated scenario is to test the orphan-removal path against rows that **never had a corresponding on-disk file** — `writeStagedProposal` would have created the file and broken the test premise. Raw SQL is correct here. Hardcoded constants + apostrophe escaping make injection risk zero. |
| 4 | AC 2-3 verified by code audit, not live soul mutation | **ACCEPT.** AC 2-3 are testing `buildCacheKey()` determinism and sensitivity. The function is pure (only reads files, hashes them, returns a string). Code audit + the type system + my own reading of the source is sufficient. A live soul mutation test would be valuable but not gating — it's the same logic as the cold/hot benchmark validation. |

---

## Risks to flag before benchmark

1. **The cache benchmark is non-hermetic.** It calls real Gemini on the cold run. If the Gemini API is slow or down at run time, the cold timing is misleading. The hot run is the actual gate (must be < 30s) and that one IS hermetic (cache hit, no Gemini). The threshold is the right thing to assert.
2. **The benchmark target agent default is `echo`.** Echo's soul + knowledge are small. A larger agent (tala, rune) might exceed 30s on hot run if there are many proposals to process through gates + judges. The target was set against a no-op rerun. **If Freddie wants to validate against tala or rune, expect to budget more headroom.** For the AC-15 sign-off, `echo` is sufficient.
3. **The pre-populated DB scenario uses `'__bench_prepop__'` as agent name.** It's not in `DISTILL_AGENTS`, so the cache benchmark won't process it. Cleanup runs in `finally`. Even if cleanup fails, the next benchmark run will `INSERT OR IGNORE` (line 85) and end up in the same state. Safe.
4. **AC 16-17 (correctness preserved across runs) are inherently probabilistic** because Gemini has temperature noise. The benchmark doesn't verify these — they require a manual before/after comparison on a live agent. Same as AAR-0007. The operator needs to eyeball one or two real runs.

---

## Recommendation: run benchmark before commit

Yes — run the benchmark before committing. Two reasons:

1. **AC-15 is a hard acceptance criterion** ("hot run < 30000ms"). It's the only way to validate the headline claim. We don't ship optimizations we can't measure (Cloudflare reflex from the task doc).
2. **The pre-populated DB scenario is also a regression guard for AAR-0008/0008B.** If the FTS5 rebuild fix breaks for any reason, this test catches it. Running it adds confidence beyond AAR-0009 itself.

**Suggested invocation:**
```
bun src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts echo
```

If echo passes, also do a quick spot run on a real agent (`tala` or `rune`) to eyeball AC-16/17 correctness — not a hard gate, but worth the 5 minutes. Don't expect those to be < 30s on the cold run.

If the benchmark passes, commit and merge. If it fails, profile which optimization underperformed (the benchmark prints both timings) and report back before shipping.

---

## Follow-ups (post-merge, not blocking)

1. **Update AAR-0009 task doc** — fix the `require("fs")` snippet to use static ESM import (matches Ryan's correction).
2. **Strengthen the prompt-version comment** in `distill-soul.ts:96-97` — add an explicit reminder that the version must also bump if `loadDir()` changes its file-formatting prefix (the cache key doesn't capture format-only changes in helper functions).
3. **Document the benchmark in `.claude/skills/dev-start/workflow.md`** as a required check before merging optimization PRs. Listed as a follow-up in the AAR-0009 failure-modes table; carry it forward.

---

## Review dimensions

### 1. Distill cache correctness

Read `src/libs/distill-cache.ts`. Verify:
- `buildCacheKey(agent: string): string` hashes soul file content + agent knowledge dir contents + shared knowledge dir contents + DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION
- Key is deterministic: same inputs → same key
- Key is sensitive: any file content change → different key
- Key includes `DISTILL_PROMPT_VERSION` from distill-soul.ts — cache invalidates when the prompt changes
- `readDistillCache(key)` returns null on: file missing, JSON parse error, version mismatch, or structural validation failure
- `writeDistillCache(key, value)` creates parent dir idempotently
- `clearDistillCache()` removes all cache files (any `--clear-cache` flag, or just the function exists for use)
- File format is JSON with fields: version, timestamp, rawOutput, inputTokens, outputTokens, cachedAt (or similar — verify matches task doc spec)

### 2. Distill cache wiring

Read `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` and `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts`. Verify:
- Cache read before `callDistillGemini` in the per-agent loop
- Cache hit: skip Gemini call, use cached rawOutput, log stderr "cache HIT" or equivalent
- Cache miss: call Gemini as before, write result to cache on success
- `--no-cache` flag on CLI bypasses read but still writes (or bypasses both — verify task doc intent)
- `--no-cache` flag is plumbed through to `autonomousDistill(agents, options)`
- Errors in cache read do NOT crash the pipeline — they fall back to Gemini call
- Errors in cache write do NOT crash the pipeline — they log and continue

### 3. Two-phase gate ordering

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `runAllGates()`. Verify:
- Phase 1: safety, size, drift run in parallel via `Promise.allSettled`
- Phase 1 failure short-circuits: phase 2 does NOT run, regression + constitution get synthetic "skipped — phase 1 failure" GateResults
- Phase 1 success: phase 2 runs (regression + constitution in parallel)
- Return value `results[]` always has 5 entries in order: safety, size, drift, regression, constitution
- Return value `failedGates[]` only includes actual failures, NOT skipped gates
- Return value `passed` is false if any actual failure, true only if all 5 actually ran and passed
- The existing AAR-0003 property "all 5 gates always run on success" is preserved
- The new behavior "expensive gates skipped on phase 1 failure" is only applied on the failure path

### 4. Rule embedding cache

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `RuleEmbeddingCache`. Verify:
- Interface has get/set semantics keyed by soul content hash (not by agent name — agent identity alone can't detect soul mutation mid-run)
- `createRuleEmbeddingCache()` factory exists
- Cache is per-run (not module-level global) — verify it's created fresh in `autonomousDistill()` and passed via `GateInput.ruleEmbeddingCache`
- `runRegressionGate()` checks cache before calling `embedBatch(rules)` — cache hit skips the embed call
- Cache key is sha256 of soul content — mutations invalidate automatically
- No stale embedding reuse across different soul versions

### 5. Batched contradiction check

Read `src/libs/gates.ts` `runRegressionGate()` and any new helper like `batchedContradictionCheck`. Verify:
- Collects all `(rule, change, similarity)` tuples where similarity > REGRESSION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
- Caps at top 20 by similarity (highest first)
- Makes ONE Gemini call with all pairs in a single prompt
- Parses response with a regex that extracts per-pair verdicts
- Fallback on parse failure: per-pair `sim > 0.92` treated as contradiction
- Fallback on Gemini API failure: same per-pair fallback
- Caller name is `gate-regression-batched` or similar (for ai-usage tracking)
- Response format: ask Gemini to emit one line per pair with PAIR-{n}: CONTRADICTION|COMPATIBLE or similar
- Edge cases: 0 pairs → skip Gemini entirely; 1 pair → still use batched format for consistency; 20+ pairs → top 20 only

### 6. Benchmark structure

Read `src/libs/__benchmarks__/autonomous-distill.bench.ts`. Verify:
- Cold scenario: cache empty, full pipeline runs
- Hot scenario: cache populated from cold run, pipeline runs again
- Hot scenario asserts `durationMs < 30000` or process.exit(1)
- Both scenarios write timing output (console or file)
- Pre-populated DB scenario: creates synthetic rows in `staged_proposals`, runs pipeline or syncStaged orphan-removal path, asserts no SQLite errors, cleans up in `finally`
- Scenarios are independent — can run in any order
- Cleanup is robust: `finally` blocks remove synthetic test agent dirs and DB rows
- File uses `__bench_prepop__` or similar synthetic agent name (doesn't collide with real agents)

### 7. AAR-0008 / AAR-0008B preservation

Grep and spot-check:
- `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts:625-626` still has the bug fix (title + proposedChange=content)
- `src/libs/staged.ts` still has parseStagedFrontmatter, fmEscape, title field
- `src/libs/brain/sync.ts` still imports parseStagedFrontmatter
- `src/libs/brain/index.ts` still has the FTS5 rebuild fix (stagedFtsNeedsRebuild + INSERT rebuild)
- Round-trip test `src/libs/__tests__/staged-roundtrip.test.ts` not modified
- Migration tool `src/tools/migrate-staged-pre-fix.ts` not modified

### 8. Correctness under optimization

This is the critical one. The optimizations MUST NOT change correctness. For each optimization:

- **Distill cache:** Cached rawOutput must be byte-identical to what Gemini would return on re-invocation IF inputs are unchanged. Verify the cache key covers EVERY input that affects Gemini output: soul content, knowledge file contents, the prompt template. Missing any one = silent stale proposal.

If agent tala and agent rune have different soul files but the cache is keyed by soul hash (not agent), they get different cache entries automatically. But verify: is the cache passed per-agent (new cache per agent) or per-run (shared across agents)? If shared, the soul-hash key handles it. If per-agent, verify it's created in the right scope.

Do NOT modify files. Do NOT touch any other code. Review only.

# AAR-0009 Code Review — McCall

**Verdict: APPROVED with notes** (2 small follow-ups, no blockers)

The implementation is faithful to the task doc, defensive in all the right places, and Ryan made three good judgment calls that I want to keep. One thing I want to flag for the operator before benchmark, and one bad-intelligence item in the task doc that I'll own.

---

## Per-dimension findings
